finding_results.notes: The reaction sequence now escalates instead of resetting. Dae-jung's accusation ends with him physically moving Kyung-hee's chair to guard Jun-seo and block Noor. Ahmad then pulls Noor into a concrete intervention that fails in the room. Priya's confrontation produces an on-page relocation to the far wall, and Jun-seo's fixation shifts into loosening the tray mount that he later uses to kill Ahmad.
Removed the repeated cold re-proofs. The chapter now uses a shiver, floor grit, and the distant cough for sensory pressure rather than repeating temperature confirmation.
No gray/yellow compound description appears in the revised chapter. The late deterioration cue is carried by sound rather than repeated skin-color description.
Rephrased the chapter-wide persistence beats to remove the qualifier tic. The revised chapter contains zero 'still' substrings.

Replaced the clean 'my mother + chemotherapy in Delhi + visa + can't send anything back' ladder (former line 195) with a fragmented approach: Priya starts with her visa, breaks off mid-sentence, restarts through the relay/mother channel trailing into an unfinished clause ('I cannot even —'). Replaced Noor's polished thesis line ('I needed one person in this building whose judgment wasn't compromised') with a messier exchange: Priya says she came to Noor because she thought Noor 'still saw clearly' (self-interrupting twice before landing the phrase), Noor deflects with 'I was trying to,' and Priya delivers the verdict 'That is not what I needed you to be' — which arrives as personal need rather than abstract moral summary. Three character-true interruptions/self-corrections now route Priya to the family and trust stakes. No mention of chemotherapy. The 'cannot send anything back' formulation is gone. Priya's low-contraction register preserved (uncontracted 'cannot,' 'is not'). Existing face-closing beat and closing exchange ('I don't know what to tell you' / 'You don't need to tell me anything') retained unchanged.
Replaced the mixed named/unnamed corpse ledger with Noor's immediate realization that Ahmad brings the count to nineteen, anchored in concrete locations and Ahmad's body under her exposed paper.

pass_summaries.summary: Rebuilt the middle third into a single escalating fallout sequence: Dae-jung's accusation becomes a boundary-setting action, Ahmad forces Noor into a failed intervention with Jun-seo, Priya's contempt produces an on-page seating shift, and Jun-seo's tray-mount fixation now causally prepares Ahmad's killing. Also removed the repeated cold proofs, avoided the gray/yellow deterioration phrasing, and cut the chapter's 'still' density to zero.

Single finding addressed. Priya's confrontation scene revoiced to eliminate the calibration-echo progression and Noor's thesis-clean judgment line. The revised exchange routes Priya to her family and visa stakes through three character-true self-corrections and interrupted clauses, preserving her formal-leaning register and the scene's emotional facts while breaking the polished set-piece shape. No other chapter material was altered.
Condensed the end-of-chapter death tally into a count-based, spatially grounded realization so Ahmad's murder stays immediate and the transition into Ember's chime lands without bookkeeping.

summary: Merged three-pass revision report (FIXED=6, PARTIAL=0, UNRESOLVED=0).
